hypothesis: If Bayesian optimization guided by the specified polymer representations (PolyNC, polyBERT, MoLFormer, MolGen-large, MiniMol) and surrogate models (Gaussian Process with Matérn-5/2, GP with deep kernel, Bayesian neural network) is used to select fragment-generated polyamines for GCMC evaluation under a fixed computational budget, then the top-performing polymers will exhibit higher CO2 adsorption capacity than those chosen by random sampling, because BO efficiently prioritizes high-adsorption candidates in the surrogate landscape.
